tavily.raw: {"query":"EU AI Act AI Omnibus provisional agreement adoption date European Parliament Council final vote 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.europarl.europa.eu/thinktank/en/document/EPRS_ATA(2026)789329","title":"Digital Omnibus on AI: Adoption in plenary | Think Tank | European Parliament","content":"# Digital Omnibus on AI: Adoption in plenary. On 7 May 2026, Parliament and Council negotiators reached a provisional agreement on the Digital Omnibus on AI – a set of amendments mainly to the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Act. The Digital Omnibus on AI postpones the application of certain parts of the AI Act, while maintaining its core provisions and risk-based approach. Parliament is due to vote on the adoption of the agreed text during the June 2026 plenary session. ## About this document. ##### Publication type. ##### Author. ##### Policy area. ## Share this page:. ### Think Tank. ### European Parliament.","score":0.9039154,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://datamatters.sidley.com/2026/06/22/eu-lawmakers-reach-provisional-agreement-to-delay-key-eu-ai-act-obligations","title":"EU Lawmakers Reach Provisional Agreement to Delay Key EU AI Act ...","content":"# EU Lawmakers Reach Provisional Agreement to Delay Key EU AI Act Obligations. On 7 May 2026, following extensive negotiations, the European Council and European Parliament reached a provisional agreement on the EU Digital Omnibus on AI (AI Omnibus) which proposes targeted amendments to the EU Artificial Intelligence Act (AI Act). The EU AI Act is the EU’s flagship framework for governing the development, deployment, and use of certain AI systems in the EU. This included the core obligations for certain “high-risk” AI systems. Through this proposal, the Commission aimed to introduce “*simplification measures to ensure timely, smooth, and proportionate implementation of certain of the AI Act’s provisions.*” Central to the proposal was a delay to the implementation timetable for “high-risk” AI systems. The European Parliament indicated in its press release that the intention is to do so before 2 August 2026, i.e., the current implementation date for the AI Act’s “high-risk” AI system rules. Delayed Application of Obligations for “High Risk” AI Systems**.","score":0.8896154,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.addleshawgoddard.com/en/insights/insights-briefings/2026/technology/ai-omnibus-provisional-agreement-changes-eu-ai-act-delayed-deadlines","title":"AI Omnibus: provisional agreement on changes to EU AI Act, including delayed deadlines | Addleshaw Goddard LLP","content":"Addleshaw Goddard LLP > Insights & News > Insights & briefings > AI Omnibus: provisional agreement on changes to EU AI Act, including delayed deadlines. # AI Omnibus: provisional agreement on changes to EU AI Act, including delayed deadlines. The European Parliament and Council have reached provisional agreement on the AI Omnibus, which was introduced by the European Commission in November 2025 and contains proposals to amend the EU AI Act. The key points are:. The AI Act’s core obligations on providers and deployers of high-risk AI systems were due to become applicable in August 2026. The Commission proposed extending the right to process special category data (as defined in the GDPR) for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction (which is currently limited to providers of high-risk AI systems where it is strictly necessary) to deployers of high-risk systems and models and providers and deployers of other types of AI systems.","score":0.8690161,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.europarl.europa.eu/committees/en/agreement-reached-on-updated-ai-rules/product-details/20260608CDT16063","title":"Agreement Reached on Updated AI Rules | Subject files | Home | IMCO","content":"# Agreement Reached on Updated AI Rules. A hand juggles a circle containing the initials AI and a figurative human head, in profile. The image is sprinkled with iluminated dots. IMCO and LIBE Members adopted by overwhelming majority the provisional agreement resulting from interinstitutional negotiations on the Digital Omnibus on AI. The co-legislators reached the agreement on 7 May 2026, aiming to make it easier for providers to comply with the AI Act while maintaining its main provisions and risk-based approach. A plenary vote is scheduled for the June session. ## Share this page:. ### Committees. ### European Parliament.","score":0.85995835,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.globalpolicywatch.com/2026/06/eu-ai-act-update-timeline-relief-targeted-simplification-and-new-prohibitions-2","title":"EU AI Act Update: Timeline Relief, Targeted Simplification, and New ...","content":"On 7 May 2026, negotiators from the Council of the European Union, the European Parliament, and the European Commission reached a provisional agreement on the terms of the Digital Omnibus on AI, marking the first set of amendments to the EU AI Act since its adoption in June 2024. * Annex III HRAIS (use-based): Obligations are postponed from 2 August 2026 to 2 December 2027 (i.e., deferred by 16 months). Transparency obligations under Article 50(2): For AI systems generating or manipulating synthetic content and placed on the EU market or put into service before 2 August 2026, the provider’s obligation to ensure that the system’s outputs are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated is postponed from 2 August 2026 to 2 December 2026 (i.e., deferred by 4 months). Now that the Machinery Regulation is listed under Section B, the full suite of Chapter III, AI Act obligations will no longer apply directly to such products; instead, the Commission must adopt delegated acts amending Annex III of the Machinery Regulation to incorporate AI-specific health and safety requirements by 2 August 2028.","score":0.85538095,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.71,"request_id":"635fce38-f62a-4bfe-ab69-c86a0ce9e460"}
